subtype_rationale: The lesion demonstrates classic features of superficial spreading melanoma with irregular radial growth pattern, multiple colors, and asymmetric pigment distribution characteristic of horizontal growth phase.
key_features_visible: Multiple colors (dark brown, light brown, black)
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: Present in both axes. Border: Highly irregular with sharp cutoff and pseudopods. Color: Variegated with at least 3-4 shades (light brown, dark brown, black, white regression). Diameter: Appears >6mm.

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows an irregular pigment network with variation in mesh thickness, peripheral fading with irregular borders, and multiple brown-red color tones suggesting radial growth phase typical of superficial spreading melanoma.
key_features_visible: Atypical pigment network with irregular mesh
Color variegation (brown, dark brown, reddish tones)
Irregular borders with pseudopods/radial streaming at periphery
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: present in both axes; Border: irregular with ill-defined edges and peripheral extensions; Color: 3+ colors including light brown, dark brown, and blue-gray; Diameter: appears >6mm based on dermoscopic field

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows characteristic features of lentigo maligna including annular-granular pattern, asymmetric pigmented follicular openings, and rhomboidal structures on sun-damaged skin with background solar lentigines.
key_features_visible: Irregular pigment network with rhomboidal structures
Background chronic sun damage with solar lentigines
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: present in both axes; Border: irregular with ill-defined edges fading into surrounding skin; Color: variegated browns and gray tones; Diameter: appears >6mm with characteristic slow radial spread pattern

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows a predominantly flat growth pattern with multiple colors and regression features (white scarring/peppering), characteristic of superficial spreading melanoma. The irregular pigment network remnants and blue-white veil areas support this classification.
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: present in both axes; Border: irregular with ill-defined edges; Color: highly variegated with brown, blue-gray, pink, and white regression areas; Diameter: appears >6mm based on image context

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows irregular pigment distribution with multiple colors spreading horizontally across the skin surface, characteristic radial growth phase pattern, and lacks the acral or facial location features of other subtypes.
key_features_visible: Multiple brown shades (light and dark brown)
Irregular borders with pseudopods/radial streaming at periphery
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: Present in both axes. Border: Irregular with indistinct edges and peripheral extension. Color: Multiple shades including light brown, dark brown, and blue-gray suggesting regression. Diameter: Appears >6mm based on image scale.

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows irregular brown pigmentation with heterogeneous coloration suggesting possible radial growth phase, though the image quality and small size limit definitive subtyping.
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: present in both axes; Border: irregular and poorly defined; Color: heterogeneous brown with darker focal areas; Diameter: appears relatively small but concerning pattern

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows predominantly pink-red coloration with minimal melanin pigmentation, polymorphous vascular pattern with dotted and linear irregular vessels, and milky-red areas characteristic of amelanotic/hypomelanotic melanoma.
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry present in both axes; borders are irregular and ill-defined; color is predominantly pink-red with subtle brown tones centrally (hypomelanotic); diameter appears >6mm. The lack of typical melanocytic features with atypical vascular pattern is concerning.

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows a relatively flat appearance with variegated brown pigmentation and an irregular network pattern, consistent with superficial spreading melanoma in radial growth phase.
key_features_visible: Atypical pigment network with irregular meshes
Multiple regression structures (white scar-like areas within lesion)
Color variegation (light and dark brown tones)
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: present in both axes; Border: irregular with indistinct edges superiorly; Color: 2-3 shades of brown with focal hypopigmented regression areas; Diameter: appears >6mm

subtype_rationale: The lesion shows an irregular pigment network with areas of regression and multiple brown dots distributed irregularly, consistent with superficial spreading melanoma in radial growth phase.
key_features_visible: Atypical pigment network with variable mesh thickness
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: Present in both axes. Border: Irregular with ill-defined edges. Color: Brown with variation in intensity, areas of lighter regression. Diameter: Appears >6mm.

subtype_rationale: This lesion appears to be a benign melanocytic nevus rather than melanoma. The features present are consistent with a common acquired nevus, not a melanoma subtype.
classic_ABCD_summary: Asymmetry: Relatively symmetric structure; Border: Reasonably regular and well-defined; Color: Homogeneous brown without concerning polychromia; Diameter: Appears small, likely <6mm
